reading, okay, and that's very pronounced, say, when you look here down at this picture here.

We can take an utterance and tell you, here's the sequence of phonemes that the person produced, but we're such fluent speakers that there aren't usually breaks between one phoneme and the next.
Sometimes there's pauses in speech. If I say the word baby, there's actually a silent period there because I close off all the flow of sound.
But in most speech sounds, there's what we call co-articulation.
Things seamlessly blend from one phoneme onto the next. And indeed, you can't really look at the spectrogram frequently.
